figure.title: Atomic Operation Operand and Return Data
figure.purpose: Shows how operand data is packed into a request and how the corresponding return data is formatted for Atomic operations.

technical_summary: Shows Operand Data for 4-byte Op1 AtomicR/AtomicNR.
Operands (E0, E1, E2, E3, E4) are placed at specific Doubleword offsets (0, 4, 6, 7, 9) corresponding to the target addresses.
Byte Enables for the operand data are 0xF (4 bytes).
Returned Data for AtomicR aligns exactly with the operand positions (Dat E0, E1, E2, E3, E4 at DW0, DW4, DW6, DW7, DW9).

packet_layout.description: Read Response Data channel carrying returned values

critical_facts: Atomic operations use the OrigData channel to send operands and the RdRspData channel to return previous values.
Data layout is sparsely packed according to the target addresses, with corresponding Byte Enables (0xF) marking the active 4-byte operands.
